description_long: This obelisk features a tall, rectangular column constructed from a material resembling weathered stone or wood, with a spiral staircase wrapping around its exterior from base to near the top. The staircase is detailed with individual steps and appears to be integrated into the main shaft. At the summit, a circular blue platform supports a large, faceted gem or crystal, which is encased by several curved metallic rods that arch upward and outward, forming a decorative cage. The gem is multicolored, with hues of green and gold, and the rods add an ornamental, almost mystical quality to the structure. The overall design combines elements of classical monumentality with fantastical, almost steampunk-inspired ornamentation.
description: A tall, rectangular obelisk with a spiral staircase, topped by a caged, multicolored gem and decorative metal rods.
description_view_0: The obelisk is seen with the spiral staircase clearly visible, and the golden side of the gem at the top is prominent within the metal cage.
description_view_1: This view highlights the green, faceted side of the gem and shows the spiral staircase from a different angle, revealing more of the blue platform at the top.
description_view_2: The staircase is seen winding up the shaft, and the gem appears more green, with the metal rods forming a symmetrical cage around it.
description_view_3: The obelisk's spiral staircase is visible from the opposite side, and the green gem is partially obscured by the metal rods, with the blue platform more apparent.
attribute_ornamentation: Decorative metal rods form a cage around the gem at the top; the spiral staircase is an ornamental architectural feature.
attribute_base_design: The base is a simple, rectangular extension of the main shaft, with no visible steps or additional platforms, and appears to be made of the same stone or wood material as the column.
attribute_tip_design: The tip features a large, faceted gem or crystal set on a blue circular platform, surrounded by several curved metallic rods that arch upward and outward, forming a decorative cage.
